text.title: Retention Analysis and Strategy Checklist
text.summary: Ordered steps for establishing retention baseline, identifying drivers, and planning improvements — from metric definition through monitoring.

list.item: Define what "active" means for this product (login, core action, transaction)
Determine retention periods: D1, D7, D30, D90
Calculate current rates; D1 benchmark 40-60%, D7 20-30%, D30 10-15%, monthly churn below 5%
Create retention cohort table segmented by acquisition source
Identify retention curve shape: steep early drop (onboarding problem) vs gradual decay (value problem)
Create retention curve visualization; mark drop-off periods (week 1, month 2)
Compare retention by user segment, channel, and persona
Find cohorts with best retention; identify what they did differently in first 7 days
Interview churned users: why did they leave?
Map journey of power users vs churners; find divergence point
List features used by retained users; correlate feature adoption with D30 retention
Identify frequency thresholds (how many sessions in week 1 predicts D30 retention?)
Identify product's primary retention loop type (content / social / progress / stored-value / habit / network)
For each core flow: map Trigger → Action → Variable Reward → Investment; mark missing components
Define key aha moments (moments correlated with long-term retention)
Design habit-forming features targeting the missing Hook components
Plan communication strategy: trigger schedule, channel mix, fatigue caps (max triggers/day)
Create win-back campaigns for lapsed users (inactivity-based triggers)
Set retention improvement targets (D30 from X% to Y% within Z cohorts)
Create retention dashboard with cohort heatmap
Set alerts for retention drops exceeding 2pp week-over-week
Track retention vs activation correlation
Monitor notification unsubscribe and uninstall rates as fatigue signals
Weekly cohort review; compare to previous cohorts and industry benchmarks
